D2 Vision L4 Analyze Reference

Bezold-Brücke Shift

slide a single wavelength brighter and watch its hue drift — except at the three invariant points

Hue is not fixed by wavelength alone. As a monochromatic light grows brighter its hue shifts toward yellow or blue — but three special wavelengths hold their hue at any intensity.

The left patch is a dim wavelength, the right the same wavelength turned up. Slide the wavelength: between the marked invariants the bright patch drifts in hue; park it on 478, 503, or 578 nm and the drift disappears.
